provenance.title: Beyond Softmax: A Natural Parameterization for Categorical Random Variables
affiliations: Università della Svizzera italiana (USI), IDSIA, Lugano, Switzerland
provenance.venue: Proceedings of the 43rd International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML 2026), Seoul, South Korea. PMLR 306.
provenance.abstract_truncated: Latent categorical variables are frequently found in deep learning architectures. We revisit the softmax function, demonstrate its limitations from an information-geometric perspective, and replace it with the catnat function—a hierarchical sequence of binary splits—that yields a diagonal Fisher Information Matrix, improving gradient-based optimization.
key_claims: The standard softmax function has a dense Fisher Information Matrix (FIM) that induces geometric distortions in parameter space.
The proposed catnat parameterization, built from hierarchical binary decisions, yields a diagonal FIM (Theorem 4.2), removing all cross-score coupling.
The natural activation function ν further simplifies the diagonal FIM entries, making them independent of the local score si (Corollary 4.3).
catnat consistently outperforms softmax and sparsemax across Graph Structure Learning (GSL), Variational Autoencoders (VAE), and Reinforcement Learning (RL) experiments.
catnat is a drop-in replacement for softmax: same API shape, compatible with existing gradient estimators and training stabilization techniques.

architecture.overview: catnat replaces the softmax mapping π: R^S → Δ^{K-1} with a hierarchical binary tree of depth H = log2(K). Each internal node applies a scalar activation a(s_i) ∈ [0,1] to produce a Bernoulli branching probability. Leaf probabilities are products of ancestor Bernoulli factors.

modules.name: Encoder (g_θ)
modules.role: Maps input x ∈ X to unnormalized scores s ∈ R^S
modules.output_shape: [B, S]  where S = K-1 for catnat (K for softmax)
modules.op_type: Neural network (task-specific; CNN for VAE, GCN for GSL, CNN for RL)

modules.name: catnat Parameterization (π)
modules.role: Maps unnormalized scores to a valid probability vector over K classes via a binary tree

modules.name: Natural Activation ν(x)
modules.role: Scalar activation applied at each internal tree node; chosen to make the diagonal FIM entries constant w.r.t. local score
modules.formula: ν(x) = 0 if x ≤ C - A/2; (1 + sin(π(x-C)/A))/2 if C-A/2 ≤ x ≤ C+A/2; 1 if x ≥ C+A/2

parameters.C: shift parameter (default 0); not a tunable hyperparameter
parameters.A: slope parameter (set to 2π so that ∂ν/∂s|_{s=0} = ∂σ/∂s|_{s=0}); not a tunable hyperparameter

modules.name: Sampler
modules.role: Samples categorical variable C from Cat(p1,...,pK)
modules.gradient_method_gsl_rl: Score Function (REINFORCE) with LOO or multi-sample baseline

modules.name: Decoder / Predictor (f_ψ)
modules.role: Task-specific network consuming (x, C) to produce output y

variants.name: catnat-ν
variants.description: catnat with natural activation ν; the recommended variant

architecture.parameter_count_note: catnat uses K-1 parameters for K classes (vs K for softmax), eliminating the overparameterization of the probability simplex.

ambiguities.description: The paper requires K to be a power of 2 for a balanced binary tree. The paper does not explicitly address non-power-of-2 K.
ambiguities.primary_assumption: K is always chosen to be a power of 2 in experiments (K ∈ {8,16,32} for VAE)

equations.name: Model Pipeline
equations.role: Defines the full inference pipeline from input to output

equations.name: Softmax FIM (Proposition 4.1)
equations.latex: G_{\text{smx}}(s)_{ij} = \begin{cases} p_i(1-p_i) & i=j \\ -p_i p_j & i \neq j \end{cases}
equations.role: Shows the dense, coupled structure of softmax's FIM — the core motivation for catnat

equations.name: catnat Leaf Probability
equations.role: Core formula: each leaf probability is a product of ancestor Bernoulli factors

equations.name: catnat FIM — Diagonal (Theorem 4.2)
equations.latex: G_a(s)_{ij} = \begin{cases} 0 & i \neq j \\ P(a_i) \left(\frac{\partial a_i}{\partial s_i}\right)^2 \frac{1}{a_i(1-a_i)} & i = j \end{cases}
equations.role: Proves catnat's FIM is diagonal, eliminating cross-score coupling

equations.name: Natural Activation Function
equations.latex: \nu(x) = \begin{cases} 0 & x \leq C - \frac{A}{2} \\ \frac{1 + \sin\!\left(\frac{\pi(x-C)}{A}\right)}{2} & C - \frac{A}{2} \leq x \leq C + \frac{A}{2} \\ 1 & x \geq C + \frac{A}{2} \end{cases}
equations.role: Activation that makes the diagonal FIM entries constant (independent of local score)

equations.name: catnat FIM with Natural Activation (Corollary 4.3)
equations.latex: G_\nu(s)_{ij} = \begin{cases} 0 & i \neq j \\ P(a_i) \cdot \frac{\pi^2}{A^2} & i = j,\ |s_i - C_i| < \frac{A}{2} \end{cases}
equations.role: Simplest diagonal FIM: each entry is proportional only to P(a_i), the probability of reaching node i

equations.name: Pulled-back FIM for catnat w.r.t. network params θ
equations.role: Shows that catnat removes all cross-score statistical coupling from the network-level FIM

equations.name: Score Function Gradient Estimator (REINFORCE with baseline)
equations.role: Used to train latent graph parameters in GSL experiment

equations.name: Energy Score Loss
equations.latex: \mathcal{L}_{\text{ES}} = \frac{1}{M}\sum_{m=1}^M \|f_\psi(x, A_m) - y\|_2 - \frac{1}{2M(M-1)} \sum_{m \neq n} \|f_\psi(x, A_m) - f_\psi(x, A_n)\|_2
equations.role: Proper scoring rule used for GSL training; multivariate extension of CRPS

equations.name: VAE ELBO
equations.role: Training objective for the categorical VAE experiment

equations.name: PPO Clipped Surrogate Objective
equations.role: RL training objective; catnat replaces softmax in the policy head only

tensors.name: s (scores)
tensors.note: K-1 scores for catnat (one per internal tree node); K scores for softmax

tensors.name: a_i (node activations)
tensors.note: Bernoulli probabilities at internal tree nodes

tensors.name: C (categorical sample)
tensors.note: One-hot in discrete case; dense relaxation for Gumbel-Softmax

tensors.name: s_VAE
tensors.note: VAE: N categorical variables each with K classes

experiments.name: Graph Structure Learning (GSL)
experiments.gradient_estimator: REINFORCE (Score Function) with Leave-One-Out (LOO) multi-sample baseline

experiments.name: Reinforcement Learning (RL)
experiments.architecture: Shared actor-critic CNN (3 conv layers + FC-512)

datasets.name: Synthetic GSL dataset
datasets.description: 10,000 (x, y) pairs generated by a GCN on a community-structured latent graph. 5 entropy settings (θ* ∈ {0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 0.9}). 80/10/10 train/val/test split.

datasets.name: Binary MNIST
datasets.description: MNIST binarized at 0.5 pixel intensity threshold

primary_results_summary.GSL: catnat-ν outperforms softmax, sparsemax, catnat-σ on all 5 entropy settings and all 4 metrics. Largest gains in MAE on θ (latent calibration).
primary_results_summary.VAE_Adam: Both catnat variants outperform softmax and sparsemax across all N∈{10,20,30} and K∈{8,16,32}. catnat-σ and catnat-ν statistically equivalent overall.
primary_results_summary.VAE_SGD: Same trend as Adam, confirming optimizer-agnosticism.

evaluation_protocol.statistical_testing: Welch's t-test, p < 0.05 for bold entries

implementation_assumptions.assumption: K must be a power of 2 to use a balanced binary tree
implementation_assumptions.basis: All experiments use K ∈ {2, 8, 16, 32}; paper does not discuss unbalanced trees
alternatives: Pad K to next power of 2
Use unbalanced tree with sentinel leaves

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Gumbel noise is applied to log-probabilities of leaf nodes p_k in the catnat case (not to node activations a_i)
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper says catnat is compatible with Gumbel-Softmax but does not specify where noise is injected
alternatives: Apply Gumbel noise to intermediate node logits

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Score initialization for GSL uses U(0, 0.1) (as stated in Appendix E.5)
VAE encoder architecture: 3 conv layers (ReLU) + 2 FC layers
Kernel sizes, strides, and channel counts for VAE conv layers are not specified; standard MNIST VAE defaults assumed
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper references open-source codebase at github.com/jxmorris12/categorical-vae
alternatives: Fetch from referenced codebase for exact configuration

implementation_assumptions.assumption: RL CNN backbone matches Mnih et al. (2013/2015) DQN architecture exactly as described: 32×8×8 stride 4, 64×4×4 stride 2, 64×3×3 stride 1, FC-512
KL divergence in VAE is computed analytically (both posterior and prior are categorical)
implementation_assumptions.basis: Stated in Appendix F.4; prior is uniform Cat(1/K,...,1/K)

ambiguities.description: The binary tree requires K to be a power of 2. The paper is silent on non-power-of-2 K.
alternatives: Pad to next power of 2 with dummy classes

ambiguities.description: Where exactly Gumbel noise is added in the catnat-Gumbel-Softmax hybrid: at leaf logits or at node activation logits.
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Gumbel noise added at leaf log-probabilities

ambiguities.description: Number of scores S: for catnat S = K-1, but the paper uses S interchangeably with K in some places.
ambiguities.primary_assumption: S = K-1 for catnat (one per internal node)
